sourceMovie.title: The Killer
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: The Killer (2023) stuck because it commits to its crime premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. David Fincher's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The crime and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: American Psycho
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The crime appeal of The Killer — that dark edge, the thriller — translates cleanly into American Psycho (2000). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Nightcrawler
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Nightcrawler (2014) clearly understood what makes crime work — the same quality that keeps The Killer rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Transporter
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Transporter (2002) is the kind of film The Killer fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The crime overlap is real, but it's the dark emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Extraction 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Extraction 2 shares The Killer's gift for crime — the 2023 film delivers thriller without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Layer Cake
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes The Killer compelling — the crime, the thriller — shows up in Layer Cake (2004) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that dark storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Memory
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The crime energy of The Killer is alive in Memory (2022), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a dark payoff that rivals the original pick.

recommendations.title: The Long Kiss Goodnight
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Long Kiss Goodnight (1996) operates in the same crime as The Killer, but with its own distinct voice and pacing. The dark payoff will feel satisfyingly familiar without retreading the same ground.

recommendations.title: The Iceman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Iceman (2012) belongs to the same conversation as The Killer — both anchor their crime in thriller emotion that doesn't let go. The dark undercurrent connects them more than any plot summary would suggest.

recommendations.title: The Lady from Shanghai
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Killer's crime stayed with you, The Lady from Shanghai (1947) delivers the same sensation through an entirely different story. The thriller through-line is the real common thread between them.

recommendations.title: Blow
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where The Killer excels in crime, Blow (2001) doubles down on drama to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Killer?
faq.answer: Try American Psycho, Nightcrawler, The Transporter—each shares DNA with The Killer while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Killer?
faq.answer: The Killer (2023) is best described as Crime, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Killer worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.6/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
